11.reason: [This condition is evaluated before checking if the surrounding characters are of the same valid type and may cause undefined behavior when i=0 (s[-1] is invalid) or when s[i+1] is '\0' (end of string). Also, it skips printing '-' when s[i+1] <= s[i-1], but according to requirements, '-' should be printed unchanged in invalid cases, including when the range is non-increasing. However, the bigger issue is potential out-of-bounds access.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition is evaluated before checking if s[i-1] and s[i+1] are valid characters of the same type. Also, when i=0, s[i-1] accesses s[-1], which is out-of-bounds and undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition is evaluated before checking if the surrounding characters are valid and of the same type. It may access s[-1] when i=0, causing undefined behavior. Also, it does not ensure both sides are of the same category (e.g., letter/digit).]
